title: Koanophyllon galeanum
intro: Koanophyllon galeanum, also known as Galeana's leaf, is a plant species within the family Asteraceae, native to Mexico and Central America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Koanophyllon galeanum include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Koanophyllon galeanum is often used in traditional medicine to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Koanophyllon galeanum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Koanophyllon and is a type of daisy. The specific classification for this species is Koanophyllon galeanum, which is a part of the larger group of flowering plants. The plant is native to Mexico and is a part of the local flora. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it shares characteristics with other daisy species.
